title: Our Father
subtitle: The Lord teaches His disciples to pray on the mountain

stillness: The strength of prayer lies in the devotion of the mind,
not in the multiplying of words.
Be still, and dare to call God Father.
gospel: Among all the things said about prayer,
the Lord set the Lord's Prayer in the midst,
Our Father, who art in heaven.
behold: Behold attentively the Lord Jesus upon the mountain,
as if you yourself were present,
shaping for His disciples this brief and blessed form of prayer.
See how He puts a few words into your mouth,
yet within them folds away immeasurable mysteries.
Hear Him give you leave to say: Our Father.
that the thing made should name its Maker,
a man should dare to call God his Father!
Who in the old Law ever dared to presume such a thing?
No one. No one at all.
consider: To the men of old God was called Lord, as to servants;
now He is called Father, whom we serve not from fear, but from love.
We, made sons by adoption through the blood of the Lord Jesus,
cry out with confidence: Abba, Father.
By this name love is kindled in us;
for what ought to be dearer to sons than a father?
And there rises a kind of bold presumption of obtaining what we ask:
for what will He not give to His sons who beg,
when He has already granted them this very thing first,
that they should be His sons?
Then consider with what care it touches the soul,
that whoever says 'Our Father' should not be unworthy of so great a Father.
He does not say 'My Father,' but 'Our Father,'
that you may pour out your prayer for the whole body,
and everywhere seek not your own good, but your neighbour's.
By this He slays enmity, and beats down pride,
and drives out envy, and brings in charity, the mother of all good things.
For one and the same nobility God has given to all,
when He has deigned to be called the Father of all.
And 'who art in heaven' — that is, in the hearts of the just,
who are the temple of God, in whom, as in His holy temple, He dwells.
Or, in the hidden depth of Thy majesty, still concealed from us:
Truly Thou art a hidden God.
prayer: O Power, O Word of wondrous working!
Come now, my good Lord Jesus, Word of the Father,
perfect Thou these words spoken by Thee and laid upon my mouth.
Perfect them, and say Amen; say, let it be done;
say to me as Thou saidst to that Syrophoenician woman:
Be it done to thee as thou wilt.
O sweet love of Jesus, O sweet truth,
O sweet Amen, O sweet word, 'let it be':
Be it done to me according to Thy word.

latinJewel.latin: O quanta fiducia et fiducialis audacia, quod factura Factorem, creatura Creatorem, homo Deum Patrem audeat nuncupare!
latinJewel.english: O what confidence, what daring trust, that the thing made should dare to name its Maker, the creature its Creator, a man should dare to call God his Father!
